compiled_state.title: Configuration refinement: align rebar config with community conventions
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context
rebar config is a BESPOKE flat key=value file (.rebar/config.conf / .rebar.conf / $REBAR_CONFIG) read by TWO independent ad-hoc inline "="-splitting parsers (_engine_support/lookups.py::_display_mode, _commands/txn.py verify-gate loop), plus ~40 distinct env vars across ~150 read sites with no single source of truth. A subagent researched what popular, actively-maintained Python CLI/dev-tools (ruff, black, mypy, pytest, pip, uv, poetry, coverage) converged on; a second subagent reviewed this epic. This aligns rebar with those conventions.

## Research-backed target (community convergence)
- FORMAT: TOML under [tool.rebar] in pyproject.toml + optional standalone rebar.toml, via stdlib tomllib (PEP 518/621/680). Replaces the bespoke flat parser (silent "="-in-value bugs, no types/nesting, silent unknown-key drop).
- DISCOVERY: walk UP from cwd to nearest project config (stop at rebar.toml / [tool.rebar] / .git / fs root); fall back to user-level XDG (~/.config/rebar/) only when no project config exists.
- PRECEDENCE (documented): CLI flag > REBAR_<KEY> env var > project config > user config > built-in defaults.
- SAFETY: single typed Config (source of truth); reject/warn on unknown keys (typo guard vs todays silent drop); fail-closed on parse error; alias old keys during a deprecation window; a rebar config/--show-config command for precedence transparency.
- SECRETS stay in env / gitignored .env (JIRA_API_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), NOT committed config.

## HARD CONSTRAINTS (rebar-specific; deviations from the research)
- Core is stdlib-only (pyproject dependencies = []): so NO pydantic-settings (use a stdlib dataclass Config) and NO platformdirs (hand-roll XDG via $XDG_CONFIG_HOME / ~/.config).
- tomllib is fine: runtime floor is ALREADY requires-python>=3.11 (the ruff target-version=py310 is only a LINT target, intentionally conservative) — NO tomli fallback / NO core dep needed.
- Config files are ordinary WORKING-TREE content on main, NOT events on the tickets branch — so there is NO event-log migration and config is not auto-pushed/merged by the store. Back-compat is plain file compat.
- Module-size policy: EXTEND the existing src/rebar/config.py (55 LOC) into the typed-Config home; do not spawn a sub-100-LOC fragment. Keep parity with / retire its bash mirror _engine/rebar-config.sh.
- Live config-file keys today are ONLY verify.require_signature_for_close (legacy alias verify.require_verdict_for_close) and ticket.display_mode (paths.*/planning.* were already removed).

## Success Criteria
- One typed Config source of truth; both inline parsers retired; no scattered ad-hoc config reads.
- TOML config + documented precedence; bespoke flat parser retired behind a back-compat alias window.
- Unknown-key + parse-error handling is loud, not silent — and the fail-closed verify gate is preserved.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR-style note (docs/) of format/precedence/discovery; confirm tomllib usable (floor already >=3.11, no fallback) — optionally bump ruff target py310->py311 as trivial cleanup
- [ ] Single typed Config (extend src/rebar/config.py, stdlib dataclass) as source of truth + validation + loud unknown-key handling
- [ ] TOML support ([tool.rebar] + rebar.toml) via tomllib, upward discovery + hand-rolled XDG user fallback (no platformdirs)
- [ ] Route ALL config-file reads through Config and RETIRE both inline parsers (lookups.py::_display_mode, txn.py verify loop)
- [ ] PRESERVE fail-closed verify gate: present-but-unreadable verify.* => require signature; absent => gate off — with a regression test
- [ ] Back-compat: old .rebar/config.conf reads IDENTICALLY for >=1 release; legacy keys aliased; unknown keys WARN (not fail) during deprecation, hard-error only after
- [ ] rebar config / --show-config: resolved values + which layer each came from
- [ ] Env-var standardization tracked as a SEPARATE linked child (REBAR_<KEY> prefixing is a breaking rename of established unprefixed vars PROJECT_ROOT/JIRA_*/TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR/... each needing an alias window) — informed by the interactive env-var review
- [ ] Docs (README/CLAUDE.md/docs) updated; full non-integration suite green

comments.body: Subagent review (2026-06-16) across convergence/maintainability/reliability/fitness/hygiene/correctness. Verdict: strong plan; applied these fixes: (1) CORRECTNESS — corrected the tomllib AC: requires-python is ALREADY >=3.11 (pyproject:16), so tomllib needs no fallback/dep; the py310 is only the ruff LINT target (pyproject:105). (2) Added a testable fail-closed AC (preserve verify-gate: present-but-unreadable=>require sig). (3) Noted config is working-tree content on main, NOT events on the tickets branch (no event-log migration). (4) Concretized back-compat (old config reads identically >=1 release; unknown keys warn->error). (5) Flagged REBAR_<KEY> as a breaking rename of unprefixed public vars (~40 vars / ~150 sites) -> spun into a separate linked story (60ce). (6) Added explicit 'retire both inline parsers' AC. (7) Added platformdirs exclusion (hand-rolled XDG, stdlib-only). (8) Module-size: extend the 55-LOC config.py, keep parity with/retire the rebar-config.sh mirror. DECOMPOSED into 7 ordered child tasks (744e ADR -> 252e Config -> {43a0 loader, fe78 route+retire+fail-closed, c647 show-config} -> 83e6 back-compat -> f81a docs) + the separate env story 60ce.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CORRECTION from the interactive env-var review: the 'live config-file keys' list (verify.require_signature_for_close, ticket.display_mode) is INCOMPLETE. At least one more exists: rebar_id_guard_mode (read by rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py via _get_rebar_id_guard_mode_from_config, env REBAR_ID_GUARD_MODE > config > default 'raise'). ACTION: the typed-Config task (252e) must EXHAUSTIVELY enumerate all live config-file keys (grep every config_file()/inline reader), not just the two known. Also, env-var review is surfacing settings to add as config keys: compact.threshold (from COMPACT_THRESHOLD).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: SCOPE ADD (user, 2026-06-16): the epic now INCLUDES performance validation of the new config functionality — child task e211-63ef-bc96-4609. Config resolution is hot-path; the typed Config + TOML + discovery + layering must be cached/bounded with no regression vs the inline parsers. (Treat as an epic acceptance gate alongside the existing ACs.)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: PROGRESS CHECKPOINT (2026-06-16, session handoff). DONE (4/9 children, all pushed to main): 744e ADR (docs/config.md), 252e typed Config (stdlib dataclass, full key inventory, coerce/validate/alias/unknown-warn), 43a0 loader (tomllib + upward/XDG discovery + sparse-merge layering CLI>env>project>user>defaults), fe78 routed the verify gate + display_mode through load_config (fail-closed preserved; FIXED a malformed-pyproject fail-OPEN; dropped DSO WORKFLOW_CONFIG_FILE; added tests/conftest XDG isolation for portability). load_config is now the unified entry point. Full offline suite green (1877). REMAINING: c647 (rebar config/--show-config), 83e6 (back-compat alias window + deprecation warnings), e211 (perf — IMPORTANT: check if _display_mode is called per-ticket in list renders; if so add memoization + dedup the pyproject double-parse), f81a (docs sweep), and the 4 env-var tasks EV-1..4 under 60ce (sync rename, dead-var removal, user-var renames, REBAR_LLM_RUNNER derivation). DEFER: SESSION_ID (83f2 needs user approval). next-batch top is EV-2 (P1) but it's a large multi-file removal; e211/c647 are more tractable. Loop assignee: config-loop.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: SESSION CODE REVIEW (config-loop, 2026-06-16, commit d76e2a31). Ran a holistic subagent review of the cumulative session diff (aa5c5fb6..; 9 commits). Verified CORRECT: _env_signature cache-key completeness (all canonical + 5 legacy aliases + policy vars), fail-closed verify gate preserved through caching/strict-keys, stdlib-only core (no new deps), no leftover refs to the 7 removed vars. Fixed 3 findings: (1) ruff — brought the whole tree back to lint+format clean (ruff check + ruff format --check both pass; the PROJECT_ROOT line-removals had left reconciler files non-canonical); (2) compact — load_config ConfigError now a clean Error+exit1, not a traceback; (3) mcp gates — readonly/allow_llm were reported by `rebar config` but read env-only; routed all 3 mcp gates through one _mcp_gate() helper on the typed config so config-file is honored and reported==enforced, with per-gate fail directions (readonly fail-CLOSED like verify; allow_llm/allow_jira_sync fail-safe off). +new tests; full offline suite green (1951). Verify gate untouched.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: OSS BEST-PRACTICES VALIDATION (config-loop, subagent + web research vs ruff/black/mypy/pytest/pip/uv/hatch/poetry/coverage/pydantic-settings + PEP 518/621/680). VERDICT: rebar config is ABOVE the OSS median — single typed resolver; principled discovery (upward walk, stops at .git, dedicated rebar.toml > pyproject [tool.rebar], explicit $REBAR_CONFIG short-circuit); documented precedence CLI>env>project>user>defaults with defaults-applied-once (a low layer default never beats a high layer value); best-in-class env aliasing (REBAR_<SECTION>_<KEY> + deprecation alias windows with canonical-wins + warn-once + the NO_SYNC negative-flip = pydantic AliasChoices pattern); staleness-safe caching (warm hit re-stats read files; errors never cached; in-process verify-gate edits honored); and a rebar config transparency command that reuses the EXACT load_config layer assembly (provenance cannot drift) — matches/exceeds ruff --show-settings / pip config debug. FAIL-CLOSED VERIFY GATE confirmed correctly implemented (present-but-unreadable verify.* selected so _read_toml_table raises -> require signature). Intentional stdlib-only deviations (hand-rolled XDG, no pydantic-settings, no platformdirs, caching) introduce NO fundamental risk. FINDINGS: (MAJOR) the cli_overrides precedence layer is built + correct but no first-class CLI flag routes through it -> follow-up ticket cdd4-4349-4534-4473 (discovered_from a621). (MINOR, FIXED this session) relative/blank XDG_CONFIG_HOME was honored (resolved vs cwd -> non-portable); now ignored per XDG spec, falls back to ~/.config + tests; also documented that rebar uses ~/.config on ALL platforms incl. macOS (deliberate, matches ruff/black/mypy; not ~/Library/Application Support). (ENHANCEMENT/intentional) unknown-key default is WARN during the deprecation window (vs ruff/cargo fail-closed) — planned cutover flips to error via REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS; no system/XDG_CONFIG_DIRS tier — intentional non-goal for a repo-scoped tool. Net: conformant + no unhandled gotchas; one feature-completeness follow-up + minor doc note for f81a (macOS line in docs/config.md).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: HANDOFF CHECKPOINT (config-loop, 2026-06-17). The epic is substantively COMPLETE: all original config-file tasks (744e ADR, 252e typed Config, 43a0 loader/discovery, fe78 route+retire-inline-parsers, c647 rebar config, 83e6 back-compat, e211 perf, f81a docs) + the env-var story 60ce (EV-1..EV-4) are CLOSED. OSS best-practices validation (vs ruff/black/mypy/pytest/pip/uv/hatch/poetry/coverage/pydantic-settings + PEP 518/621/680) rated it ABOVE the OSS median; fail-closed verify gate confirmed correct. Two discovered follow-ups: cdd4 (wire CLI precedence layer via git -c style "rebar -c SECTION.KEY=VALUE") = CLOSED; 0ac6 (route reconciler.*/jira.*/llm.* config-FILE keys + ticket_clarity through the typed Config) = IN PROGRESS (slice 1/4 done: ticket_clarity). USER DECISION: implement 0ac6 fully, THEN close a621. So a621 should be closed by the agent that finishes 0ac6 — see the 0ac6 HANDOFF CHECKPOINT for the remaining slices (reconciler/jira/llm) + design guidance. State: main @ 2f8faa9d, store clean, full offline suite green (1995).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
EPIC COMPLETE (config-loop). Configuration refinement is fully landed and the last child (0ac6) is closed, per the user decision "implement 0ac6, THEN close a621".

WHAT SHIPPED (all 11 children closed):
- 744e ADR (docs/config.md design-of-record); 252e typed Config (stdlib dataclass, full key inventory, coerce/validate/alias/loud-unknown-key); 43a0 loader (tomllib + upward discovery + hand-rolled XDG + sparse-merge layering); fe78 routed the verify gate + ticket.display_mode through load_config and RETIRED both inline parsers (fail-closed verify gate preserved); c647 `rebar config` transparency; 83e6 back-compat alias window; e211 perf (cached/bounded resolution, no hot-path regression); f81a docs sweep.
- Env-var standardization story 60ce (EV-1..EV-4): REBAR_<KEY> standardization with deprecation alias windows, dead-var removal, REBAR_LLM_RUNNER derivation.
- Two discovered follow-ups: cdd4 (the `rebar -c SECTION.KEY=VALUE` git -c-style CLI precedence layer) CLOSED; 0ac6 (route reconciler.*/jira.*/llm.* config-FILE keys + ticket_clarity through the typed Config) CLOSED — its 4 slices: ticket_clarity (2f8faa9d), reconciler.* (8b4d797f), jira.* (287788df, also LIVE-validated against the Jira DIG project), llm.* (48783029), docs (25da9388).

RESULT vs the epic's Success/Acceptance Criteria:
- ONE typed Config source of truth; ALL config-file reads route through load_config (the inline parsers are retired; the last bespoke .rebar/config.conf reads — ticket_clarity + rebar_id_guard_mode — are folded into the typed loader). llm.* is the one deliberate exception (reserved section resolved by LLMConfig.from_env so the stdlib core never imports the agents extra) but still uses the core discovery.
- TOML ([tool.rebar] + rebar.toml) via tomllib, upward discovery + XDG user fallback (no platformdirs); documented precedence CLI > REBAR_<KEY> env > project > user > defaults, enforced by the single resolver + the `rebar -c` flag.
- Loud unknown-key handling (warn → error via REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS); fail-CLOSED verify gate preserved + regression-tested; back-compat: legacy .rebar/config.conf + every renamed env var honored as a deprecated alias with a warning.
- stdlib-only core (dependencies = []; tomllib only, no pydantic-settings/platformdirs); config files are working-tree content, not events.
- OSS best-practices validation (vs ruff/black/mypy/pytest/pip/uv/hatch/poetry/coverage/pydantic-settings + PEP 518/621/680) rated rebar's config ABOVE the OSS median.

VERIFICATION: full offline suite green throughout (final 2049 passed); ruff check+format clean; per-slice subagent reviews clean; jira.* additionally validated LIVE against DIG (reconcile dry-run + link round-trip). Store health clean (fsck + validate).

Remaining non-blocking follow-up (NOT part of this epic): 3b92 — route bridge_fsck's one out-of-scope AcliClient site through the jira config layer.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
